cve_2020_11984_proof.explanation: v1.1 requires all patch families and all selected prerequisites in one global group. The 2.4.x activation/fix and trunk prerequisite/fix are DAG-divergent. No release tag can satisfy both branch-local boundary conditions and both fixes, so the global state produces an empty prediction.

fix_commits.subject: Always correct the width and height of the image (#1859).

current_judgments.reasoning_short: Sink anchor calling SetImageExtent; move_copy_blame differs (53004b14d), so introduction origin is ambiguous between this commit and the move source; normal and ignore-whitespace blames agree on 8c69a644. Ancestor of fix, survives to fix parent, same file touch, and root cause predicate binding all support boundary relevance. Release reachability too broad across 72 tags is noted but does not change boundary semantics. Move/copy conflict unresolved, hence uncertain.

Control predecessor NULL-check on decode_options is a required precondition enabling the vulnerable path; all SZZ blame variants agree (stable_blame_variants). Ancestor of fix, survives to fix parent, same file touch, root cause predicate binding. Candidate_in_fix_series_hint noted but does not override the clear prerequisite semantics from anchor role and line content. This alone never activates the vulnerability.

fix_commits.subject: target/arm: Fix PAuth sbox functions
fix_commits.body:
In the PAC computation, sbox was applied over wrong bits.
As this is a 4-bit sbox, bit index should be incremented by 4 instead of 16.

Test vector from QARMA paper (https://eprint.iacr.org/2016/444.pdf) was
used to verify one computation of the pauth_computepac() function which
uses sbox2.

Track our recursion depth in fAbstractSyntaxNType. It calls several
functions which in turn call it, which makes it easy to overflow the

current_judgments.reasoning_short: Data-source function declaration (static int, line ~14074) is a prerequisite providing input to the recursive processing path but does not activate unbounded recursion. Distant from recursion loci (~7972-7990). Stable SZZ blame across all variants but extensive release reachability (370 tags, 21 lines) and release_line_overreach flag reduce boundary specificity to low confidence.

Control-predecessor while-loop (line 7990) drives repeated entry into fAbstractSyntaxNType, activating the unbounded recursion path. Semantically central to the vulnerability's trigger mechanism. Move-copy SZZ variant disagrees (move_copy_blame=6de0c799, canonical=63e31768), creating blame-origin uncertainty that prevents confident selection despite the clear activation role.

Recursion-entry function fAbstractSyntaxNType (line 7972) introduces the self-recursive call structure that creates unbounded recursion. Whitespace-sensitive blame disagreement (normal=59277781, ignore_whitespace=809fb769) and candidate_in_fix_series_hint flag suggest possible fix-series contamination, undermining confidence in this commit as the authentic introduction point.

fix_commits.subject: ati-vga: Fix checks in ati_2d_blt() to avoid crash
fix_commits.body:
In some corner cases (that never happen during normal operation but a
malicious guest could program wrong values) pixman functions were
called with parameters that result in a crash. Fix this and add more
checks to disallow such cases.

fix_commits.subject: block/iscsi:fix heap-buffer-overflow in iscsi_aio_ioctl_cb
fix_commits.body:
There is an overflow, the source 'datain.data[2]' is 100 bytes,
 but the 'ss' is 252 bytes.This may cause a security issue because
 we can access a lot of unrelated memory data.

The len for sbp copy data should take the minimum of mx_sb_len and
 sb_len_wr, not the maximum.

If we use iscsi device for VM backend storage, ASAN show stack:

fix_commits.subject: Merge r1880205, r1880214 from trunk:
fix_commits.body:
mod_proxy_uwsgi: Error out on HTTP header larger than 16K

The uwsgi protocol does not let us serialize more than 16K of HTTP header,
so fail early with 500 if it happens.


Follow up to r1880205, APLOGNO().

current_judgments.reasoning_short: Declares pktsize as apr_uint16_t, directly matching root cause predicate VP-1 and fix predicate FP-1. Stable blame with all variants agreeing (variant_agreement=all_same). This is the type declaration prerequisite that enables integer overflow; does not itself trigger overflow but is required for it to occur.

Assignment pktsize = headerlen - 4 activates integer overflow when headerlen exceeds apr_uint16_t range. Stable blame (all_same), ancestor of fix, survives to fix parent, 14 reachable release tags. Bound to VP-1 with dangerous_use_role features; non_release_tag_noise flagged but weak.

Same logical line as candidate 99b806cf (pktsize = headerlen - 4) but from different patch family with whitespace_differs blame: canonical blame resolves to 7a6038c9 not da54e90d. Whitespace sensitivity cannot be resolved; redundant with stable-blame activation candidate. Rejected as equivalent_fix_noise.

fix_commits.subject: Merge r1688399 from trunk:
fix_commits.body:
mod_remoteip: Use r->useragent_addr as the root trusted address for verifying.

This fixes issue resulting in setting of bad useragent_ip when internal
redirection has been generated as response to the request (typically as
result of "ErrorDocument 40x").

In this case, the original request has been handled by mod_remoteip and its
useragent_ip has been changed properly, but when internal redirection
to ErrorDocument has been generated later, the mod_remoteip's handler has been
executed again with *the same* c->client_addr as in the original request. If
c->client_addr IP is trusted, this results in bad useragent_ip being set.

When using r->useragent_addr as the root trusted address instead of
c->client_addr, the internal redirection uses the first non-trusted
IP in this particular case, so it won't change the r->useragent_ip during
the internal redirection to ErrorDocument.

Submitted by: jkaluza
Reviewed/backported by: jim

SZZ all variants agree on commit 354b4b74, candidate is ancestor of fix, line survives to fix parent. No blame conflicts (all_same), no merge risk (merge_candidate=false). Fix predicate fp-1 also touches this line (adding depth parameter). No blame conflicts (all_same), no merge risk. The function definition without depth tracking is a required container for the recursive call but does not itself activate the vulnerability; it is a prerequisite.

SZZ all_same with matching commit, stable_blame_variants, line survives to fix parent. Fallback evidence, weak root cause binding, and release_reachability_too_broad (280 tags) preclude selected. Activation is most plausible role but cannot be confirmed without attacker context.

Trivial structural brace '} else {' deleted as part of fix code reorganization. SZZ blame conflict: candidate_commit_sha 84465f218 disagrees with SZZ canonical_blame 513a431e despite all_same variant agreement, indicating blame origin ambiguity. direct_deleted_line with stable_blame_variants but line text carries no semantic vulnerability content. Release reachability too broad (280 tags). Rejected as refactor_noise.

av_probe_input_buffer call with pls->segments[0]->url at line 1633, dangerous_use anchor, modified_old_side. Fix alters this probing call where UAF may manifest when operating on a freed or broken playlist. SZZ all_same matches candidate commit, stable_blame_variants, line survives to fix parent. Fallback evidence, weak root cause binding, broad_candidate_range, release_reachability_too_broad (161 tags) prevent selected. Activation is most plausible role as the call site where freed memory access occurs, but attacker context unavailable.

Conditional check 'av_strstart(line, "#", NULL))' deleted by fix as part of HLS parsing logic restructuring. direct_deleted_line with dangerous_use role but line is a structural parsing conditional, not a vulnerability-introducing construct. SZZ all_same matches candidate commit, stable_blame_variants. broad_candidate_range, release_reachability_too_broad (280 tags), weak_root_cause_binding. Rejected as refactor_noise from fix code reorganization.

pls->broken = 1 sets broken flag without proper guards, missing_guard_target with add_only_semantic_target. Fix adds guards around broken state handling. Setting broken creates necessary precondition for UAF (playlist marked broken then accessed without null checks) but does not itself free memory or dereference freed pointer — actual UAF activation occurs downstream. SZZ all_same matches, stable_blame_variants, release_reachability not too broad (1 tag). Fallback evidence, weak_root_cause_binding, and no attacker context prevent selected.

fix_commits.subject: Fix NULL dereference in SSL_check_chain() for TLS 1.3
fix_commits.body:
In the tls1_check_sig_alg() helper function, we loop through the list of
"signature_algorithms_cert" values received from the client and attempt
to look up each one in turn in our internal table that maps wire
codepoint to string-form name, digest and/or signature NID, etc., in
order to compare the signature scheme from the peer's list against what
is used to sign the certificates in the certificate chain we're
checking.  Unfortunately, when the peer sends a value that we don't
support, the lookup returns NULL, but we unconditionally dereference the
lookup result for the comparison, leading to an application crash
triggerable by an unauthenticated client.

Since we will not be able to say anything about algorithms we don't
recognize, treat NULL return from lookup as "does not match".

We currently only apply the "signature_algorithm_cert" checks on TLS 1.3
connections, so previous TLS versions are unaffected.  SSL_check_chain()
is not called directly from libssl, but may be used by the application
inside a callback (e.g., client_hello or cert callback) to verify that a
candidate certificate chain will be acceptable to the client.

CVE-2020-1967

Reviewed-by: Matt Caswell <matt@openssl.org>

fix_commits.subject: KVM: SEV: add cache flush to solve SEV cache incoherency issues
fix_commits.body:
Flush the CPU caches when memory is reclaimed from an SEV guest (where
reclaim also includes it being unmapped from KVM's memslots).  Due to lack
of coherency for SEV encrypted memory, failure to flush results in silent
data corruption if userspace is malicious/broken and doesn't ensure SEV
guest memory is properly pinned and unpinned.

Cache coherency is not enforced across the VM boundary in SEV (AMD APM
vol.2 Section 15.34.7). Confidential cachelines, generated by confidential
VM guests have to be explicitly flushed on the host side. If a memory page
containing dirty confidential cachelines was released by VM and reallocated
to another user, the cachelines may corrupt the new user at a later time.

KVM takes a shortcut by assuming all confidential memory remain pinned
until the end of VM lifetime. Therefore, KVM does not flush cache at
mmu_notifier invalidation events. Because of this incorrect assumption and
the lack of cache flushing, malicous userspace can crash the host kernel:
creating a malicious VM and continuously allocates/releases unpinned
confidential memory pages when the VM is running.

Add cache flush operations to mmu_notifier operations to ensure that any
physical memory leaving the guest VM get flushed. In particular, hook
mmu_notifier_invalidate_range_start and mmu_notifier_release events and
flush cache accordingly. The hook after releasing the mmu lock to avoid
contention with other vCPUs.

Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
Suggested-by: Sean Christpherson <seanjc@google.com>
Reported-by: Mingwei Zhang <mizhang@google.com>
Signed-off-by: Mingwei Zhang <mizhang@google.com>
Message-Id: <20220421031407.2516575-4-mizhang@google.com>
Signed-off-by: Paolo Bonzini <pbonzini@redhat.com>
changed_function_hints: int sev_vm_move_enc_context_from(struct kvm *kvm, unsigned int source_fd);
typedef void (*on_lock_fn_t)(struct kvm *kvm, unsigned long start,

current_judgments.reasoning_short: Control predecessor if (locked) gate in kvm_mmu_notifier_invalidate_range_start with modified_old_side anchor and stable blame variants across all SZZ modes. Gates the MMU notifier path through which SEV guest memory reclamation notifications flow; generic KVM infrastructure prerequisite that enables the vulnerable code path but does not itself introduce the SEV cache-flush omission.

Function declaration of kvm_mmu_notifier_invalidate_range_start selected via context_fallback with add_only_semantic_anchor. Move/copy blame diverges (93065ac753e4 vs canonical f922bd9b), creating ancestry uncertainty. Structural prerequisite providing the MMU notifier callback entry point but no direct role in SEV cache-flush vulnerability introduction.

fix_commits.subject: vfs: fs_context: fix up param length parsing in legacy_parse_param
fix_commits.body:
The "PAGE_SIZE - 2 - size" calculation in legacy_parse_param() is an
unsigned type so a large value of "size" results in a high positive
value instead of a negative value as expected.  Fix this by getting rid
of the subtraction.

fix_commits.subject: bpf: Fix kernel address leakage in atomic fetch
fix_commits.body:
The change in commit 37086bfdc737 ("bpf: Propagate stack bounds to registers
in atomics w/ BPF_FETCH") around check_mem_access() handling is buggy since
this would allow for unprivileged users to leak kernel pointers. For example,
an atomic fetch/and with -1 on a stack destination which holds a spilled
pointer will migrate the spilled register type into a scalar, which can then
be exported out of the program (since scalar != pointer) by dumping it into
a map value.

The original implementation of XADD was preventing this situation by using
a double call to check_mem_access() one with BPF_READ and a subsequent one
with BPF_WRITE, in both cases passing -1 as a placeholder value instead of
register as per XADD semantics since it didn't contain a value fetch. The
BPF_READ also included a check in check_stack_read_fixed_off() which rejects
the program if the stack slot is of __is_pointer_value() if dst_regno < 0.
The latter is to distinguish whether we're dealing with a regular stack spill/
fill or some arithmetical operation which is disallowed on non-scalars, see
also 6e7e63cbb023 ("bpf: Forbid XADD on spilled pointers for unprivileged
users") for more context on check_mem_access() and its handling of placeholder
value -1.

One minimally intrusive option to fix the leak is for the BPF_FETCH case to
initially check the BPF_READ case via check_mem_access() with -1 as register,
followed by the actual load case with non-negative load_reg to propagate
stack bounds to registers.

Fixes: 37086bfdc737 ("bpf: Propagate stack bounds to registers in atomics w/ BPF_FETCH")
Reported-by: <n4ke4mry@gmail.com>
Acked-by: Brendan Jackman <jackmanb@google.com>
Signed-off-by: Daniel Borkmann <daniel@iogearbox.net>
Signed-off-by: Alexei Starovoitov <ast@kernel.org>
changed_function_hints: static int check_atomic(struct bpf_verifier_env *env, int insn_idx, struct bpf_i

current_judgments.reasoning_short: SZZ all_same variant agreement with stable_blame_variants; candidate is ancestor of fix and line survives to fix_parent; root_cause_predicate_bound and modified_old_side_anchor on dangerous_use; release reachability covers 4 tags without too_broad flag. No conflicting blame, merge, or non-ancestor risk. non_release_tag_noise does not contradict boundary evidence.

fix_commits.subject: bonding: fix null dereference in bond_ipsec_add_sa()
fix_commits.body:
If bond doesn't have real device, bond->curr_active_slave is null.
But bond_ipsec_add_sa() dereferences bond->curr_active_slave without
null checking.
So, null-ptr-deref would occur.

Test commands:
    ip link add bond0 type bond
    ip link set bond0 up

0x44434241343332312423222114131211f4f3f2f1 128 sel src 14.0.0.52/24 \
dst 14.0.0.70/24 proto tcp offload dev bond0 dir in

fix_commits.subject: sctp: account stream padding length for reconf chunk
fix_commits.body:
sctp_make_strreset_req() makes repeated calls to sctp_addto_chunk()
which will automatically account for padding on each call. inreq and
outreq are already 4 bytes aligned, but the payload is not and doing
SCTP_PAD4(a + b) (which _sctp_make_chunk() did implicitly here) is
different from SCTP_PAD4(a) + SCTP_PAD4(b) and not enough. It led to
possible attempt to use more buffer than it was allocated and triggered
a BUG_ON.

Cc: Vlad Yasevich <vyasevich@gmail.com>
Cc: Neil Horman <nhorman@tuxdriver.com>
Cc: Greg KH <gregkh@linuxfoundation.org>
Fixes: cc16f00f6529 ("sctp: add support for generating stream reconf ssn reset request chunk")
